url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 3-4 suggested queries + competitor-drift-watchpoint flags (e.g. £10k abandoned threshold on MTD picks; net-profit-based threshold logic on A18; alarmist/breezy tonal-register on A17 LPC rescue).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §19 (MTD ITSA Wave 3 + Wave 4 extensions §19.1-§19.17) + §27 (HMRC enquiry + Sch 41 / Sch 24 / Sch 55 / Sch 56 + F-5 lock on Sch 24 no-12-month-qualifier vs Sch 41 para 13 12-month qualifier + CoP9 vs DDS vs LPC routing) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-1-adjacent risk (legacy 31/46/91 Sch 56 day-triggers crossed with new 3%/3%/10% Spring Statement 2025 percentages) carried verbatim across A13 (SA penalty regime) + A16 (system overhaul). FA 2021 Sch 24 (new MTD points regime) vs FA 2007 Sch 24 (long-standing inaccuracy regime) numerical-name collision flagged as Stage 2 drift watchpoint across A13 + A16. F-5 Wave 7 lock (Sch 41 para 13 has 12-month qualifier on non-deliberate-unprompted 0% floor; Sch 24 para 10 has NO equivalent 12-month qualifier on careless-unprompted floor) carried verbatim across A13 + A14 + A15 + A17. £10,000 abandoned MTD ITSA threshold (§19.8 Wave 3 lock) carried as do-not-write watchpoint across A16 + A18.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified FA 2009 Sch 55 + Sch 56 / FA 2021 Sch 24 (points regime) / SI 2021/1076 regs 4/8/11/16/20/21/22 / TMA 1970 Sch A1 / F(No.2)A 2017 Sch 14 / TMA 1970 s.7/s.8/s.28A/s.29/s.31A/s.34/s.36/s.36A/s.59A/s.59B/s.86 / Sch 41 FA 2008 paras 5/6A/13/20 / Sch 24 FA 2007 / Spring Statement 2025 HTML 3%/3%/10% on 15/30/31 day-triggers / Perrin v HMRC [2018] UKUT 156 / HMRC v Tooth [2021] UKSC 17 / HMRC v Hicks [2020] UKUT 12 / Martland v HMRC [2018] UKUT 178 anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §19 (full Wave 3 + Wave 4 extensions) + §27 (Wave 7 lock) locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed in M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 + M3-B-B1 D-2). Each extended brief landed at +138 to +151 lines of additional content (worked examples 4-6 per pick + 12 FAQs per pick + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). Cross-bucket-cluster pattern across A-B1 + A-B2 + A-B3 + B-B1 confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_LPC_CLUSTER_DEPTH_DISCIPLINE — picks 2 (A14 calculator), 3 (A15 strategic-rationale), 5 (A17 emotional-rescue) form a deliberate 3-page LPC sub-cluster within batch M3-A-B3, each targeting a distinct intent / tonal register against the broader LPC architecture (A12 LPC orientation hub completed in M3-A-B2 batch; A3 benefits-tally + A2 DDS umbrella in prior waves). Stage 2 anti-templating notes for all three picks explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk + the structurally-distinct frames: A14 is calculator-tool / numerical-estimation; A15 is strategic-philosophical (five-strand rationale: policy-design / time-value-of-certainty / asymmetric-downside / route-architecture / downstream-engagement-cost); A17 is emotional-rescue (five-strand structure: acknowledgement / architectural-reassurance / immediate-action / operational-cycle / specialist-routing) with calm-structural-action-oriented tonal register. RUN session pairing of these three picks should hold the line on per-intent / per-frame distinctness.

D-4 STAGE2_MTD_BIFURCATION_PAIR_DISCIPLINE — picks 4 (A16 system-overhaul orientation) and 6 (A18 qualifying-income deep-dive) form a deliberate 2-page MTD sub-cluster within batch M3-A-B3, each targeting a distinct intent against the broader MTD architecture (A6 news-framing + A7 exit-mechanic + A11 LtdCo + A19 joint-owner + A20 penalty-catalogue in prior batches or prior waves). Stage 2 anti-templating notes for both picks explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk + structurally-distinct frames: A16 is orientation-level four-axis cultural-shift narrative (filing frequency / record-form / software / agent route); A18 is technical-input deep-dive on qualifying-income definition (gross-not-net headline + aggregation + exclusions + traps + boundaries). RUN session pairing should hold the line on orientation-vs-deep-dive distinction.

D-5 STAGE2_F1_F5_CROSS_PICK_CONSISTENCY — picks 1 (A13 SA penalty regime) + 4 (A16 MTD overhaul) both depend on F-1-adjacent risk (legacy 31/46/91 day-triggers crossed with new 3%/3%/10% percentages) + FA 2021 Sch 24 vs FA 2007 Sch 24 numerical-name collision. Stage 2 brief extensions carry both watchpoints verbatim. Picks 1 (A13) + 2 (A14) + 3 (A15) + 5 (A17) all depend on F-5 Wave 7 lock (Sch 41 para 13 12-month qualifier vs Sch 24 para 10 no-12-month-qualifier). Stage 2 brief extensions carry F-5 verbatim. Cross-pick consistency anchor for RUN session: pages writing must hold these distinctions consistently across the LPC + MTD + penalty clusters.

D-6 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-B-B1 D-5 patterns (~2 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-7 STAGE2_CALCULATOR_COMPONENT_NOTE — pick 2 (A14 LPC penalty calculator) requires actual JavaScript calculator component implementation at RUN time (likely React at `Property/web/src/components/calculators/LpcPenaltyCalculator.tsx`), not narrative description. The 18-cell input grid (3 behaviour bands × 2 prompted/unprompted × 3 territory categories) + early-notify-advantage line + counterfactual line + specialist-routing trigger is the canonical structure. Stage 2 added a calculator-component implementation step at step 8 of the 19-step workflow specifically for this pick. RUN session should test the calculator across the 18 cells before publishing.

D-8 STAGE2_CROSS_BUCKET_CONSISTENCY_NOTE — picks 1 (A13 SA penalty regime) + 4 (A16 MTD overhaul) + 6 (A18 qualifying-income) directly reference §19.7 penalty-cascade content; the bifurcation across legacy + MTD regimes runs through all three pages. RUN session should ensure the 15/30/31 day-triggers + 3%/3%/10% percentages are stated identically across the three pages (and never paired with the legacy 31/46/91 day-triggers — the F-1-adjacent risk).
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: A13 SA penalty regime (vs A1 PAYE-regime / A5 CT-regime / A20 MTD-catalogue; F-1-adjacent + FA 2021/FA 2007 Sch 24 collision); A14 LPC calculator (vs A12 LPC-orientation / A15 strategic-rationale / A17 emotional-rescue / A3 benefits-tally / A2 DDS umbrella; calculator IS the differentiator); A15 LPC strategic-rationale (vs A3 tactical-benefits / A12 orientation / A14 calculator / A17 rescue; depth-of-frame is the differentiator — strategic-philosophical not tactical-numerical); A16 MTD system-overhaul (vs A6 news / A7 exit / A11 LtdCo / A18 qualifying-income / A19 joint-owner / A20 penalty-catalogue; four-axis orientation frame is the differentiator; £10k abandoned threshold called out); A17 LPC emotional-rescue (vs A12 orientation / A14 calculator / A15 strategic / A3 benefits / A2 DDS; calm-structural-action-oriented tonal register is the differentiator; LPC-vs-CoP9 boundary routing); A18 qualifying-income (vs A6 / A7 / A11 / A16 / A19 / A20; gross-not-net headline + technical-definition deep-dive is the differentiator). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname distinct per pick (A13: Pemberton/Whitfield/Cordwell/Marwood; A14: Hollingsworth/Rashleigh/Belmount/Vandenberg/Padworth; A15: Stratton/Underhill/Westhampnett/Hollybank/Penbury; A16: Ainsworth/Browning/Crowther/Davenport; A17: Kettering/Linstead/Mortlake/Norcombe/Ottershaw; A18: Pevensey/Quenington/Rochester/Stapleford/Thirling/Underbrook). 28 distinct anonymised personas across 6 picks; no surname collisions within batch.
